questions.question: What is the official title of the head of the Civil Service Commission?
questions.explanation: The Civil Service Commission (CSC) is headed by a Chairperson and two Commissioners. The Chairperson serves as the chief executive officer of the CSC.

questions.question: Republic Act 6713, known as the Code of Conduct and Ethical Standards for Public Officials and Employees, mandates that all public officials file a Statement of Assets, Liabilities, and Net Worth (SALN):
questions.explanation: Under RA 6713, public officials and employees are required to file their SALN annually, within 30 days after assumption of office and within 30 days after expiration of term or separation from service.

questions.question: The ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations) was established in:
questions.explanation: ASEAN was established on August 8, 1967, through the Bangkok Declaration signed by the five founding members: Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines, Singapore, and Thailand.

questions.question: Who is the appointing authority for career positions in the Philippine government?

choices.D: The appointing officer designated by law

questions.explanation: Under civil service law, appointments to career positions are made by the proper appointing authority as designated by law, which varies depending on the position and agency.

questions.question: Under the Anti-Red Tape Act (RA 11032), what is the maximum number of working days to act on simple transactions?
questions.explanation: Under RA 11032 (Ease of Doing Business Act), government agencies must act on simple transactions within 3 working days, complex transactions within 7 working days, and highly technical transactions within 20 working days.

questions.question: The Philippine national flower is the:
questions.explanation: The Sampaguita (Jasminum sambac) is the national flower of the Philippines. It was declared the national flower on February 1, 1934, through Proclamation No. 652.

questions.question: Which law institutionalized the Government Internship Program for fresh graduates?
questions.explanation: Republic Act 7041, also known as the Government Internship Law, provides for a government internship program to provide practical training to deserving college graduates who want to enter government service.

questions.question: The merit and fitness principle in the Philippine civil service means that government positions must be filled based on:

choices.B: Seniority and length of service only

questions.explanation: The merit and fitness principle, enshrined in the Philippine Constitution and Civil Service Law, mandates that appointments in the civil service shall be made only according to merit and fitness to be determined, as far as practicable, by competitive examination.

questions.question: The Philippine Constitution was ratified in:
questions.explanation: The current 1987 Philippine Constitution was ratified on February 2, 1987, through a national plebiscite, replacing the 1973 Constitution which was in effect during the Marcos era.

questions.question: Under RA 6713, public officials are prohibited from engaging in private business during office hours. This prohibition is known as:
questions.explanation: The conflict of interest rule under RA 6713 prohibits public officials from having financial or other personal interests that may conflict with the discharge of their duties. Engaging in private business during official hours is one manifestation of conflict of interest.
